steps.title: Recognize the gap: current barrier vs. needed solutions
steps.content: The passage's key signal is the explicit contrast: “not yet commercially viable as a large-scale energy source” immediately followed by “To meet this challenge, scientists from many fields are researching ways to...” This structure—identifying a barrier, then describing research efforts to overcome it—anchors the main idea.

steps.title: Match the gap to the correct answer
steps.content:
Choice A directly identifies what the passage emphasizes: continued research is necessary before commercial viability and widespread use can occur.

The other three fail by either reversing the “not yet viable” statement (B) or focusing on isolated technical details rather than the central adoption barrier (C and D).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
